provisions.title: IV
provisions.raw_text:
SEC. 2401. 
Budgetary effects
(a) 
Statutory paygo scorecards
The budgetary effects of this division shall not be entered on either PAYGO scorecard maintained pursuant to section 4(d) of the Statutory Pay-As-You-Go
notes: Standard budget-neutrality provision exempting Division B from PAYGO and budget scoring requirements
Subsection (a) exempts from Statutory PAYGO scorecards (section 4(d) of P.L. 111-139)
Subsection (b) exempts from Senate PAYGO scorecards (H. Con. Res. 71, 115th Congress)
Subsection (c) exempts from classification under Budget Scorekeeping Guidelines and BBEDCA section 251, CBA section 302(a) allocations, and Statutory PAYGO Act section 3(4)(C)

SEC. 101(2) section 521(d)(1) substitution has no identifiable account name from the bill text alone
Multiple supplemental amounts from Divisions M and N of P.L. 117-328 are significantly reduced: total reductions exceed $2.5 billion across 10 cr_substitutions in SEC. 101
SEC. 2101(a)(2) contains an indefinite appropriation ('an amount equal to the pro rata portion of the amount appropriated for those activities for fiscal year 2023') with no specific dollar figure.
SEC. 2101(b) has an open-ended extension period that ends only when a new agreement enters into force.

All dollar amounts in Title II are authorization levels amending existing authorizing statutes (mandatory spending/contract authority from the Airport and Airway Trust Fund), not discretionary appropriations

SEC. 2307 raw text contains apparent drafting error: '21 U.S.C. 21 U.S.C. 379j–11' (duplicated citation)
No direct appropriations in Division B Title III — all provisions are amendments to authorizing statutes reauthorizing FDA user fee programs (ADUFA and AGDUFA) for FY2024–FY2028
ADUFA annual fee revenue target: $33,500,000/year for FY2024–FY2028
AGDUFA annual fee revenue target: $25,000,000/year for FY2024–FY2028 (increased from $18,336,340)
New $50,000 generic investigational new animal drug file fee established in SEC. 2312(c)
SEC. 2342 reduces Medicaid Improvement Fund from $7,000,000,000 to $6,357,117,810, a net reduction of $642,882,190 used as a budget offset
SEC. 2351 appropriates 'such sums as may be necessary'—amount is indefinite
SEC. 2352 and SEC. 2353 appropriate pro rata portions of FY2023 levels—exact amounts indeterminate from bill text alone
All mandatory spending extensions in this title are for the CR period ending November 17, 2023, except AGDUFA reauthorization (through 2028) and MIF reduction (no end date)
